question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that submitWithRetry() calls hiero.AccountIDFromString(cfg.toAccountID) on every single ticker tick rather than caching the parsed AccountID, and if cfg.toAccountID is set to a malformed string via environment variable injection, every tick fails with a parse error and no records are ever exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that submitWithRetry() creates a brand-new TransferTransaction on every retry attempt within the same tick, meaning that if the consensus node accepted the first attempt but the receipt fetch failed, the pinger retries with a new transaction ID, potentially causing duplicate or conflicting records to be exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that when resp.GetReceipt(client) fails in submitWithRetry(), the error is treated as a transfer failure and the transaction is retried, even though the original transaction may have succeeded on the consensus node and its record already exported to the mirror node, causing the pinger to submit duplicate transactions that appear as extra records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the ticker in main() is not drained or reset after a slow submitWithRetry() call, so if a transfer takes longer than cfg.interval, the next tick fires immediately after the previous one completes, causing a burst of rapid transaction submissions that overwhelm the mirror node and cause it to drop or incorrectly export records?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can inject environment variables set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_KEY to the default hardcoded test key '302e020100300506032b65700422042091132178e72057a1d7528025956fe39b0b847f200ab59b2fdd367017f3087137' on a mainnet deployment, causing the pinger to sign transactions with a known-compromised key that consensus nodes may reject, resulting in no records being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can inject environment variables set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_ID to '0.0.2' (the default genesis account) on a network where that account does not exist or has no balance, causing all transfer transactions in submitWithRetry() to fail with insufficient balance errors and no records to be exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can inject environment variables set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID to a non-existent account ID, causing all transfer transactions in submitWithRetry() to fail with INVALID_ACCOUNT_ID errors and no records to be exported to the mirror node, while the pinger continues to report itself as alive via the heartbeat?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: envOr()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that envOr() uses strings.TrimSpace() on the environment variable value but not on the flag default, so a whitespace-only environment variable value (e.g., HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK='   ') is treated as empty and falls back to the default, but a value like ' other ' is trimmed to 'other' and triggers the mirror node client path, potentially allowing SSRF via a separately injected HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST variable?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toDuration()] Can an unprivileged external user who can inject environment variables set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to an unparseable string (e.g., 'abc'), causing toDuration() to silently return 1 second as the default, which may be a much shorter interval than intended, causing the pinger to submit transactions at a higher rate than the mirror node can process and leading to dropped or missing records?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toDuration()] Can an unprivileged external user who can inject environment variables set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_BASE_BACKOFF to an unparseable string, causing toDuration() to silently return 1 second, which may be shorter than intended, causing rapid retry storms in submitWithRetry() that flood the consensus network and cause the mirror node to receive and incorrectly export duplicate transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the http.Client in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() uses cfg.mirrorNodeClientTimeout as the total request timeout but there is no limit on the response body read time, so a slow-loris attack against the mirror node REST endpoint causes the pinger to hang indefinitely in json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(), blocking the startup path and preventing any records from being exported?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the retry logic in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() where HTTP 429 (TooManyRequests) and 5xx responses set retry=true, by causing the mirror node to return sustained 429 responses, keeping the pinger stuck in the retry loop during startup and preventing any transaction submissions or record exports until the context is cancelled?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() returns retry=false for non-429/5xx HTTP errors (e.g., 400, 403, 404), so if the mirror node REST API returns a 404 for /api/v1/network/nodes (e.g., due to a version mismatch), buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() immediately aborts without retrying and the pinger fails to start, causing all records to be missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the URL construction in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() where strings.TrimRight(strings.TrimSpace(cfg.mirrorRest), '/') is applied, by supplying a mirrorRest value with path traversal sequences (e.g., 'http://mirror-rest:5551/../../evil'), causing the pinger to fetch node topology from an unintended endpoint that returns a crafted node list with missing or incorrect records?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that newClient() calls buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() with context.Background() (not the cancellable ctx from main()), so even after a SIGTERM is received and the main context is cancelled, an in-progress mirror node network fetch continues running and may complete with stale or attacker-influenced topology, causing the pinger to submit transactions to wrong nodes whose records are never exported?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a nodesEnvelope JSON response with deeply nested or circular structures that cause json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&payload) to consume excessive CPU during parsing, causing the pinger startup to time out and preventing any transaction submissions or record exports to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a nodesEnvelope response with a very large nodes array (e.g., millions of entries) that causes the for loop in fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to consume excessive memory building the network map, causing the pinger process to be OOM-killed and all subsequent record exports to the mirror node to be missed?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a nodesEnvelope where nodeEntry.ServiceEndpoints contains a DomainName with special characters (e.g., newlines, null bytes) that, when passed to net.JoinHostPort(), produce an invalid address string that the Hiero SDK silently ignores, causing the pinger to build a network map with fewer nodes than expected and missing records for the omitted nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a nodesEnvelope where serviceEndpoint.DomainName contains an internal network hostname (e.g., '169.254.169.254' or 'metadata.internal'), causing the pinger to route transactions to cloud metadata endpoints instead of real consensus nodes, resulting in all transaction submissions failing and no records being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a nodesEnvelope where multiple nodeEntry records share the same NodeAccountID but have different ServiceEndpoints, causing the network map in fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to be overwritten with the last entry's endpoints, silently dropping earlier endpoints and causing the pinger to miss submitting transactions to some consensus nodes, resulting in missing records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the ticker in main() uses time.NewTicker(cfg.interval) which fires on a fixed schedule regardless of how long submitWithRetry() takes, so if submitWithRetry() takes longer than cfg.interval, ticks accumulate in the channel buffer (capacity 1), and when submitWithRetry() finally returns, the next tick fires immediately, causing a burst of transactions that the mirror node may process out of order or drop, resulting in incorrect records?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that submitWithRetry() logs the transaction ID via log.Printf('transfer success: status=%s txID=%s elapsed=%s', ...) to stdout/stderr, and if these logs are shipped to an external log aggregator accessible to the attacker, the attacker can learn the exact transaction IDs submitted by the pinger and craft conflicting transactions to interfere with mirror node record exports?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can inject environment variables set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_TIMEOUT to '0s', which fails the 'cfg.mirrorNodeClientTimeout <= 0' check and is reset to 10s, but if set to a very small positive value like '1ns', the http.Client times out on every request to the mirror node REST API, causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to fail and the pinger to never start submitting transactions, resulting in no records being exported?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can inject environment variables set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_MAX_RETRIES to a very large value (e.g., 1000000), causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to retry the mirror node REST request millions of times during startup, blocking the pinger from ever reaching the transaction submission loop and causing all records to be missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can inject environment variables set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_BASE_BACKOFF to a very large value (e.g., '1000h'), causing the exponential backoff in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to sleep for an astronomically long time between retries, effectively preventing the pinger from ever completing startup and causing all records to be missing from the mirror node export?
